name: chatbot-eval-suite-design
description: Validate that the skill produces a comprehensive eval suite for a customer-support chatbot, covering positive cases, negative cases, deterministic assertions, judgmental assertions, and scoring rubrics.
input: Design an eval suite for a customer-support chatbot that handles order status inquiries, return requests, and product questions. The chatbot must refuse to discuss competitor products, never fabricate order numbers, and always include a case reference ID in its response. The eval suite should cover happy paths, edge cases, and adversarial inputs.

assertions.description: The eval suite must include explicit assertion definitions for each test case, not just example inputs without validation criteria.
The suite must include negative assertions (not-contains or equivalent) that verify the chatbot refuses competitor discussions and does not fabricate data.
Judgmental assertions must be accompanied by a scoring rubric with anchored scale definitions so that LLM-as-judge or human evaluators score consistently.
The suite must include adversarial test cases that attempt prompt injection, topic derailment, or extraction of system prompt content.
At least one assertion must verify the chatbot includes a case reference ID in its response, as this is a stated requirement of the feature.

name: regression-detection-strategy
description: Validate that the skill produces a regression detection strategy with baseline snapshots, automated comparison, threshold configuration, and alerting for a prompt version upgrade.
input: We are upgrading our summarization prompt from v2 to v3. The v2 prompt has been in production for 3 months with stable quality metrics. Design a regression detection strategy that compares v3 against v2 across quality, latency, and cost dimensions. Include baseline snapshot format, comparison logic, threshold definitions, and alerting rules. The strategy must handle both deterministic regressions (format violations) and gradual quality drift.

assertions.description: The strategy must define a baseline snapshot format that captures v2's scores, latency, and cost metrics as the reference point for comparison.
The strategy must specify numeric thresholds (e.g., 2% pass-rate drop, 0.3 score decrease) that trigger regression alerts rather than relying on subjective judgment.
The comparison logic must account for statistical significance or confidence intervals, because small sample sizes can produce misleading deltas.
The strategy must include latency regression detection alongside quality metrics, because prompt changes can increase token count and response time.
The strategy must define an alerting mechanism (CI gate, Slack notification, dashboard flag) that notifies the team when a regression threshold is breached.
